source_intake_record.raw_question_text: An ant crawls carefully around the edges of a cube, starting at point $P$ and in the direction of the arrow. At the end of the first edge he chooses to go either left or right. He then turns the other way at the end of the next edge and continues like this, turning right or left alternately at the end of each successive edge. After how many edges does the ant return to point $P$ for the first time? <image1>

image_dependency: Question refers to point P, an arrow, and left/right turns on the edges of a cube, which depend on the diagram
Image asset exists and registry_passed=true, so the visual reference is available
multi_step: Solving requires tracking movement edge by edge on a cube graph rather than reading off a single fact
The ant alternates left and right turns, so the path must be updated across multiple successive decisions
The question asks for the first return to P, which adds a cycle-detection/counting step
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single number
A proposed solution can be checked by explicitly tracing the ant's path on the cube
Question and answer are present and asset registry passed without issues

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产齐全，registry_passed=true 且无 issues
虽然存在 diagram-dependent orientation 风险，但该题所需图像已存在，核心语义可支撑求解与校验
答案为数值型且可通过在立方体路径上显式追踪进行验证，适合进入候选池
